memoryArtifacts.description: Alnwick Castle is the 2nd largest castle in England, & has been the home of the Percy's, Earls, & Dukes of Northumberland.

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary: William de Percy was born in 1112 at of Topcliffe, Yorkshire, England. He married Adeliza (Alice) de Tunbridge, daughter of Gilbert de Clare, 2nd Earl of Clare, Lord of Cardigan & Tonbridge and Adeliza de Clermont, before 1136; They had 1 son (Alan) & 2 daughters (Maud, wife of William, 3rd Earl of Warwick; & Agnes, wife of Joscelin de Louvain). William de Percy married Sibyl de Valoines, daughter of Thebaud de Valoines, between 1164 and 1166. William de Percy died after 1175 at of Kildare, Ireland; He was buried at Fountains Abbey. He also had 3 illegitimate sons (William; Walter; & Henry) & 2 illegitimate daughters (Alice, mistress of Henry du Puiset, and wife of Richard de Moreville; & Emma, wife of William Malebisse).

zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Percy Lord of Topcliffe於1112年出生在英國越可爾托普可利福時，他的父親William de Percy III24歲，母親Alice de Ros23歲。他於1166年與Sybil de Valognes在英國英格蘭結婚。他去世於1175年在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾福泰斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Percy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Old French percehaie ‘pierce hedge’ (Old French percer ‘to pierce, penetrate’ + haie ‘hedge, fence’), perhaps with the sense of someone breaking into an enclosure. Percehaie is the name of one of the sons of the foxes Renart and Hermeline in the medieval French epic Roman de Renart, whose earliest known version is from the 1170s. The surname is older than that, but it may originate in a nickname for the fox as ‘enclosure piercer’, perhaps amounting to ‘chicken thief’.

habitational name from any of several places called Percy in Calvados, Eure, and Manche; William de Perci, the Domesday tenant-in-chief and under-tenant of Hugh, Earl of Chester, came from either Percy-en-Auge (Eure) or Percy (Manche).

variants.name: Piercey
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Percy  .

variants.name: Piercy
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Percy  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Percy Lord of Topcliffe was born in 1112, in Topcliffe, Yorkshire, England, his father, William de Percy III, was 24 and his mother, Alice de Ros, was 23. He married Sybil de Valognes in 1166, in England, United Kingdom. He died in 1175, in County Kildare, Ireland, at the age of 63, and was buried in Fountains Abbey, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Percy Lord of Topcliffe est né en 1112, à Topcliffe, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William de Percy III, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Alice de Ros, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Sybil de Valognes en 1166, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1175, à County Kildare, Irlande, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Fountains Abbey, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: William de Percy Lord of Topcliffe(이)가 1112년 영국 요르크실르 토p클리프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Percy III(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Ros(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1166년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sybil de Valognes(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1175년 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 포우테인스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

ru.Fountains Abbey, North Yorkshire, Englan: фоунтеинс эбби, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Percy Lord of Topcliffe родился в 1112 г. в местности топклифф, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Percy III было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Ros, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Sybil de Valognes в 1166 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. Он умер в 1175 г. в местности коунти каилдар, Ирландия в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности фоунтеинс эбби, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
